description: Testable hypotheses for quality improvement, ordered by expected impact
analysis_basis: 42 iterations of parameter sweep + statistical correlation analysis

hypotheses.claim: tex_rescale=0.95 is consistently the best rescale value
hypotheses.rationale: Correlation r=+0.822 with overall score across 42 iterations. Higher rescale means less CFG over-correction, preserving natural texture variance.
hypotheses.risk: None — this is the strongest finding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: r=+0.822, strongest single parameter. Also correlates with silhouette (+0.838) and artifacts (+0.808).

hypotheses.claim: tex_guidance=8.0 with rescale=0.95 is globally optimal for avg score
hypotheses.rationale: Iter 24 achieved avg=50.0, highest of all 42 iterations. The trend shows higher guidance + high rescale = higher score.
hypotheses.risk: May not be optimal for individual examples (Ex4 does better with rescale=0.5)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed globally best avg. But Ex4 (shoes) peaks at different config (rescale=0.5). Subject-dependent optimal exists.

hypotheses.claim: Shape guidance parameters have negligible impact on scores
hypotheses.rationale: Phase 3 sweep (iter 25-41) showed near-zero correlation between shape_guidance_strength/rescale and any score dimension. All shape variations scored within ±1.5 of baseline.
hypotheses.risk: May be due to Stage 1 sparse structure dominating shape quality (immutable coords)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: shape guidance params have r<0.1 correlation with all score dimensions. Shape is determined by Stage 1 (sparse structure), not Stage 2.

hypotheses.claim: tex_guidance > 8.0 (e.g., 10.0, 12.0) with rescale=0.95 will further improve scores
hypotheses.rationale: The trend from g=3→8 with rescale=0.95 shows monotonic improvement: 48.0→47.6→47.3→48.2→48.8→50.0. Extrapolation suggests g=10-12 may continue this trend.
hypotheses.risk: Diminishing returns or reversal at very high guidance. CFG can cause mode collapse.

hypotheses.claim: Increasing ss_sampling_steps (16/20/24) will improve silhouette scores
hypotheses.rationale: Silhouette is the weakest dimension (avg ~27/100). Stage 1 sparse structure determines occupancy. More sampling steps = better occupancy prediction. Currently fixed at 12.
hypotheses.risk: ~2x compute time per extra step set. Diminishing returns likely.

hypotheses.rationale: Sparse structure guidance is currently 7.5. Since shape SLAT guidance had no effect, the bottleneck is likely Stage 1. Higher SS guidance may produce sharper occupancy grid.
hypotheses.risk: May cause over-segmentation or spurious voxels

hypotheses.claim: tex_slat_sampling_steps increase (16/20) improves detail and color
hypotheses.rationale: Detail scores are low (avg ~26/100). More texture sampling steps allow finer detail in the flow matching trajectory. Currently fixed at 12.
hypotheses.risk: Slower generation (~33-67% more time). Marginal returns possible.

hypotheses.claim: tex_rescale_t > 3.0 (e.g., 5.0, 7.0) with high guidance may help
hypotheses.rationale: rescale_t controls at which timestep CFG rescaling kicks in. Higher values apply rescaling earlier (more of the trajectory). Currently 3.0 for all stages.
hypotheses.risk: Unknown interaction — could be neutral or negative

hypotheses.claim: Seed diversity will reveal score variance and true config ranking
hypotheses.rationale: All 42 iterations used seed=42. Score differences of <1.5 points may be within natural variance. Need to test top configs with multiple seeds.
hypotheses.risk: May show that top configs are not statistically different

hypotheses.claim: Combined optimization: best tex params + high SS guidance + more SS steps
hypotheses.rationale: If H7 (higher tex_g), H8 (more SS steps), and H9 (higher SS guidance) each contribute independently, combining them should give additive improvement.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: +3-8 points overall if effects are additive
hypotheses.risk: Interactions may not be additive; combined changes may conflict

hypotheses.claim: APG (Adaptive Projected Guidance) will reduce oversaturation and dark artifacts
hypotheses.rationale: APG (Disney Research, ICLR 2025) decomposes CFG delta into parallel (causes oversaturation) and orthogonal (enhances quality) components. Down-weighting the parallel component should reduce the dark spots and saturation artifacts seen at high guidance (tex_g=5-8). Applies to all three stages (SS, shape, texture).
hypotheses.expected_improvement: +2-5 points on artifacts and color, especially for dark subjects (Ex6, Ex7)
hypotheses.risk: Low — same compute cost, easy A/B test, backward compatible

hypotheses.claim: CFG-Zero* s* projection + zero-init will improve flow matching trajectory
hypotheses.rationale: CFG-Zero* (arXiv 2503.18886) uses adaptive projection scalar s* = (v_cond^T v_uncond)/||v_uncond||^2 and skips first 1-2 ODE steps where velocity estimates are unreliable. Shown to improve FID from 2.23 to 2.10 on ImageNet. Should help early-step stability for all three stages.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: +1-3 points on silhouette and contour from better trajectory initialization
hypotheses.risk: Low — zero-init can be disabled per-step, s* is parametric

hypotheses.claim: APG + CFG-Zero* zero-init combined may give additive improvements
hypotheses.rationale: APG addresses guidance direction (parallel suppression) while zero-init addresses trajectory initialization. These are orthogonal mechanisms and may combine additively.
hypotheses.risk: Unknown interaction — combined changes may not be additive

hypotheses.claim: Beta-shaped guidance schedule will improve texture quality over binary on/off interval
hypotheses.rationale: Binary guidance interval applies full CFG abruptly within [t_start, t_end]. A smooth beta distribution curve provides gradual ramp-up and ramp-down, reducing artifacts from sudden CFG transitions. Three variants test different peaks: early (a=2,b=5 → peak at 20%, good for coarse structure), symmetric (a=2,b=2 → peak at 50%, balanced), late (a=3,b=2 → peak at 66%, emphasizes detail/texture refinement phase).
hypotheses.expected_improvement: +1-3 points on artifacts and detail from smoother CFG transitions
hypotheses.risk: Low — falls back to binary (default), zero compute overhead, same total guidance budget
